codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 6 PROPOSED_FIX items and 3 OBSERVATION_ONLY items. After classification: (1) '; echo 0' on expected-0 checks — explicitly authorized by iteration 3 dispatch directive; (2) AC4 not self-contained — dev execution concern, GIVEN clause governs preconditions; (3) AC4 additive-only check weakness — OBSERVATION_ONLY; (4) --no-index recommendation — dev implementation guidance; (5) AC5 commit-order ambiguity — Technical Hints section resolves ambiguous dependency listing; (6) on-disk report still shows iter 2 fail — resolved by writing this report. No Codex finding introduces a new blocking spec_text_vs_execution_drift.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex finding 6 resolved by writing this iteration 3 report. All other PROPOSED_FIX findings classified as OBSERVATION_ONLY with rationale. No verdict change.

observations_only.observation: '; echo 0' on expected-0 checks (AC2 cmd[1]/cmd[2], AC3 cmd[0], AC5 cmd[0]/cmd[1]) makes those checks unfalsifiable. Iteration 3 dispatch explicitly authorized retaining '; echo 0' for exit_code:0 cases.
AC4 check is not self-contained: it reads /tmp/test-scores.json but does not invoke score-update.sh. GIVEN clause governs preconditions. Dev execution concern.
AC4 additive-only claim (existing history not rewritten) is not directly verified by check commands which only print last entry's two fields.
git check-ignore --no-index would provide rule-level verification independent of git index state. Dev implementation guidance.

AC5 HEAD^..HEAD assumes spec commitment is the last commit. Ticket line 29 ('can go in same commit') is ambiguous, but Technical Hints section authoritatively says 'Each should be a separate commit'. AC is consistent with Technical Hints.
AC1/AC2 git check-ignore can false-pass after git add -f because check-ignore exits 1 for tracked files. Using --no-index tests the rule itself independently.
observations_only.classification: OBSERVATION_ONLY — dev implementation risk, not BA analysis quality failure
